headline: Malaysia's Trade Surplus Narrows to RM98B in 2025
context: DOSM reported Malaysia's trade surplus narrowed to RM98.4 billion in 2025 from RM132 billion in 2024, as semiconductor export growth slowed and imports of capital goods surged for data centre construction.

cards.big: Trade surplus shrank RM34 billion in one year — semiconductor slowdown exposes export concentration risk
cards.sub: E&E products at 42% of exports means any global chip demand wobble directly hits Malaysia's trade balance

cards.big: Data centre imports of RM28 billion in servers and cooling equipment widened the capital goods deficit by 45%
cards.sub: Short-term import surge may reverse as construction phase ends, but structural E&E export dependency remains

cards.big: China overtook Singapore as Malaysia's top trading partner at RM452 billion — 18.8% of total trade
cards.sub: Geopolitical US-China tensions create forced-choice risk for Malaysia's largest export market relationships

cards.big: MATRADE's export diversification programme spent RM2.4 billion since 2020 but top 5 products still dominate 68% of exports
cards.sub: Halal export target of RM56 billion by 2025 achieved only RM34 billion — 39% shortfall

cards.big: A shrinking trade surplus is the economy's warning light — ignore it and the current account flips red
Should Malaysia set a national target to reduce E&E export dependency below 30% of total exports by 2030?
